common.retryLatestOnly: Retry is only available for the latest assistant reply
chat.placeholder.voiceActive: Add a message or wait for transcription…
chat.selectOrCreate: Select a conversation or create a new one
chat.selectOrCreateSub: Siena will not create chats on startup or reload. Use + when you want a fresh chat.
settings.appearance.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.desc: Customize how Siena looks and feels. Changes apply immediately and persist across restarts.
settings.appearance.showTypingAnimationS: The bouncing thinking indicator while Siena is generating a reply.
settings.appearance.copyBeforeClearSub: Copies the visible chat to your clipboard before New chat clears it.
settings.model.title: Model settings
settings.model.desc: Configure local inference and active model.
settings.model.manualSwitchNote: Manual active chat switching lives in the Models screen and is validated by /api/models/active.
settings.startup.title: Startup & preload
settings.startup.desc: Startup page is real and persisted; preload/warmup/launch-at-login are not.
settings.startup.deferredBanner: Deferred — preload, warmup, and launch-at-login are not toggles here; they need a main-process Electron API (app.setLoginItemSettings) that the renderer can't safely reach without introducing an IPC bridge, which is out of scope for this pass.
settings.startup.preloadWarmupDesc: Splash readiness already uses real backend/runtime/conversation/model/settings checks — there's no fake warmup step to configure.
settings.startup.launchAtLoginDesc: electron/main.cjs has no preload script or IPC bridge today (contextIsolation on, nodeIntegration off, sandboxed) — adding one just for this one toggle was judged out of scope for this pass.
settings.tools.title: Tool permissions
settings.tools.desc: Control which optional AI capabilities Siena can use.
settings.tools.ocrSub: glm-ocr — runs on every image attachment
settings.tools.visionSub: qwen2.5vl — only runs when you ask what an image shows
settings.tools.translatorSub: translategemma:4b — powers the per-message Translate button
settings.tools.reviewerSub: Routes explicit review/critique requests to the reviewer specialist model
settings.tools.routingNote: Code specialist auto-routing lives in the Code section below. Manual model switching (qwen3.5:27b) lives in the Models screen — never auto-selected regardless of these toggles.
settings.tools.capabilityBanner: Tool access is controlled by the backend's registered-tool list and the feature flags above — not by switches in the UI. There is no separate file system / network / memory permission layer.
settings.tools.managedNote: Managed by runtime/tool registry — not configurable here yet.
settings.tools.savedLive: Saved — takes effect on the next message, no restart needed.
settings.code.title: Code rendering
settings.code.desc: Real code-specialist routing, plus syntax highlighting/code block display preferences.
settings.code.autoRoute: Auto-route coding requests to the code specialist
settings.code.autoRouteSub: Model: {model} — change the model itself in Model settings
settings.code.savedLive: Saved — takes effect on the next message, no restart needed.
settings.code.wordWrapSub: Off keeps the existing horizontal-scroll behavior for long lines.
settings.code.saveButtonSub: Downloads the code block as a local file — a real client-side export, no backend involved.
settings.code.applyRemovedNote: Apply-patch was removed — Siena has no file-editing target to apply a patch to, so that button never did anything real.
settings.voice.title: Voice
settings.voice.desc: STT (whisper.cpp) and TTS (qwen3_tts_ggml_vulkan) are real — this section exposes only the safe, already-supported defaults.
settings.voice.defaultRecognitionLanguag: Used by push-to-talk and Conversation Mode whenever no language is set explicitly
settings.voice.autoSpeak: Auto-speak new assistant replies by default
settings.voice.autoSpeakSub: Frontend preference, saved locally in this app — does not affect push-to-talk, Conversation Mode, or the manual Speak/Stream buttons
settings.voice.showStreamButtonSub: Stream is experimental: raw PCM streaming, qwen3_tts_ggml_vulkan only, no Silero fallback. This only hides/shows the button — it never changes backend behavior.
settings.voice.profileNote: Real, persisted server-side (storage/voice_profiles.json) — activating a profile changes Speak/Stream output immediately, no restart needed.
settings.language.title: Language
settings.language.desc: Input language feeds STT/language detection; response language is a soft preference injected into the chat prompt — never a hard override.
settings.language.banner: Siena v2 is optimised for multilingual interaction. Priority languages: Russian and English. Defaults ("auto") preserve Siena's current natural Russian conversation behavior exactly.
settings.language.interfaceDesc: Controls the application UI text only — separate from the input/response language preferences below.
settings.language.preferredInputSub: Same setting as Voice > Speech-to-text — used by whisper.cpp STT
settings.language.preferredResponseSub: Soft preference injected into the chat prompt — Siena still follows explicit requests and code as normal
settings.language.presetsNote: Sets the two real fields above together.
settings.language.translatorDupNote: Enable/disable Translator lives in Tool permissions above (enable_translator) — not duplicated here.
settings.language.sourceTargetNote: Source/target language: the per-message Translate button always auto-detects source and picks the opposite of Russian/English as target — there's no separate override to configure.
settings.language.preserveFormattingSub: Keep Markdown/code structure intact when translating — sent with every Translate button click
settings.presence.title: Presence
settings.presence.desc: A lightweight, local indicator of what Siena is doing right now. Not a chatbot feature — it never calls the model on its own.
settings.presence.enableSub: Turns the whole layer on/off, including the sidebar card and the status endpoint.
settings.presence.showCardSub: Shows the small status card in the sidebar. Has no effect if Presence itself is disabled above.
settings.presence.allowProactiveSub: Off by default — Siena never creates chat messages on her own unless you turn this on.
settings.presence.idleMinutesSub: How long without interaction before Presence shows as idle.
settings.presence.maxMessagesPerHourSub: Throttles both proactive messages and the manual "Say something" button.
settings.presence.quietHoursSub: Automatically treats Presence as quiet during this daily window.
settings.presence.showWelcomeBackSub: Shows a short greeting in the Presence Card when you return after being away — never a chat message, never spoken aloud.
settings.presence.showRecentEventSub: The "Latest event" block in the Presence Card (welcome back / Say something lines).
settings.presence.allowInsertToChatSub: Lets an event line be inserted into the composer. Insert only — you still press Send yourself.
settings.presence.minSecondsBetweenSub: Minimum pause between presence UI messages, so a flaky idle boundary can't spam the card.
presence.card.title: Siena is here
presence.state.available: I'm here if you need me
presence.state.idle: Around, just quiet for a moment
presence.say.throttled: Not now — quiet for a bit
presence.state.available.calm: I'm here if you need me
presence.state.quiet.playful: Staying quiet, not in your way.
presence.event.welcome_back.calm.1: I'm here if you want to continue.
presence.event.welcome_back.calm.2: Welcome back. We can continue when you're ready.
presence.event.welcome_back.playful.0: Oh, you're back! I'm right here.
presence.event.welcome_back.playful.2: There you are. I didn't go anywhere.
presence.event.say.calm.0: I'm here if you need me.
presence.event.say.calm.2: No rush — just here with you.
presence.event.say.playful.1: Just dropping by to say hi.
settings.developer.title: Developer
settings.developer.logLevelSub: Threshold for console output only — the JSONL trace log always records every event regardless of this setting
settings.developer.electronBanner: Electron integration / Local API below are read-only diagnostics, not configurable controls — the previous versions were decorative toggles/inputs that didn't do anything.
settings.developer.ipcBridgeValue: none — renderer talks to the backend only via HTTP/WebSocket
settings.developer.devToolsValue: via SIENA_OPEN_DEVTOOLS env var, not a UI toggle
settings.developer.verboseLoggingNote: Verbose debug logging is the Console log level control above (log_level=debug) — not a separate switch.
settings.developer.localApiNote: The backend has no configurable port or authentication — it's a local-only HTTP server trusted by this single desktop app, not something to edit here.
debug.subtitle: Advanced inspection workspace - Siena v2
debug.runtime.title: Runtime diagnostics
debug.runtime.viewFull: See the Runtime page for full detail.
debug.export.title: Diagnostic export
debug.export.desc: Collects app version, backend/settings status, recent errors, and a safe trace subset — no secrets, no full conversation history, no attachment contents.
debug.errors.title: Recent errors
debug.lastRequest.title: Last request
debug.lastRequest.empty: No chat request yet this session.
debug.memory.note: Memory extraction pipeline internals are not exposed as a structured debug payload yet.
debug.toolCalls.empty: No tool calls yet this session.
